reforms.description: The Italian government has introduced a wide array of measures to address low innovation and knowledge-based capital, particularly among small and medium enterprises, including the development of the venture capital industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering the venture capital industry, the reform aims to enhance funding for innovative startups, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has recently introduced a wide array of measures addressing these problems.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing the innovation ecosystem in Italy, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government has recently introduced a wide array of measures addressing these problems.

reforms.description: The Italian government plans to evaluate the effectiveness of recently introduced research and development tax credits and other fiscal incentives to improve innovation outcomes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Evaluating and potentially improving R&D tax credits can enhance their effectiveness, leading to increased investment in innovation and higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to evaluate the effectiveness of recently introduced research and development tax credits.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the effectiveness of existing innovation instruments, which can lead to better outcomes in the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Evaluate the effectiveness of recently introduced research and development tax credits and other fiscal incentives in terms of innovation outcomes.

reforms.description: In 2016, Italy's Ministry of Education, University and Research published the National Research Programme to establish a national governance system for research and innovation policies, aiming to improve coherence and reduce fragmentation in the innovation landscape.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving coherence in research and innovation policies, the reform is expected to enhance the efficiency of public R&D spending and foster innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Research Programme was published and aims to guide research and innovation policies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in Italy's approach to research and innovation, addressing long-standing fragmentation and aiming for a unified strategy.
reforms.source_quote: The overarching goal of the Programme is to establish a national governance system to guide research and innovation policies.

reforms.description: The Italian government introduced a tax exemption for revenue derived from venture capital funds that meet specific investment criteria, aimed at encouraging investment in innovative SMEs.
reforms.component_name: Tax Exemption for Venture Capital Funds
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This tax incentive is designed to stimulate private investment in innovative firms, thereby enhancing their growth prospects and contributing to economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax exemption for venture capital funds has been established and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the tax exemption is part of a broader initiative to enhance venture capital investment, making it a notable but not transformative reform.
reforms.source_quote: Tax exemption for revenue deriving from venture capital funds which respects some requisite (at least 75% of fund’s capital must be invested in not-listed SMEs).

reforms.description: The 2017 Budget Law introduced additional funding of EUR 1.5 million yearly for five years for the best university departments, aimed at enhancing the quality of the research system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases public investment in higher education, which can enhance research quality and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding was introduced as part of the 2017 Budget Law.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in funding aimed at improving research quality, although it is not a structural overhaul of the funding system.
reforms.source_quote: The 2017 Budget Law introduced additional funding of EUR 1.5 million yearly for five years for the best departments.

reforms.description: Italy introduced R&D tax credits and a patent box to enhance business-oriented science, technology, and innovation policies, although the impact on SMEs is yet to be estimated.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentives are designed to stimulate R&D investment among firms, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: R&D tax credits and the patent box have been introduced.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of R&D tax credits and a patent box represents a significant shift in Italy's approach to fostering innovation and supporting businesses, particularly SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: R&D tax credits have been introduced along with the patent box, although their impact on SMEs have yet to be estimated.

reforms.description: The Italian government has implemented a hyper-depreciation scheme for investments in Industry 4.0 technologies, allowing firms to deduct 250% of the value of their investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This scheme incentivizes firms to invest in advanced technologies, which can enhance productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The hyper-depreciation scheme was introduced with the budget law of 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: The hyper-depreciation scheme is a significant incentive for firms to invest in new technologies, contributing to the digitalization of the industrial sector.
reforms.source_quote: Hyper-depreciation scheme equivalent to 250% of the value of investments in Industry 4.0 technologies.

reforms.description: The Italian government has made permanent tax incentives for the repatriation of researchers, aimed at attracting talent back to Italy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting skilled researchers back to Italy can enhance the country's innovation capacity and contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Tax incentives on the repatriation of researchers have been made permanent.
reforms.importance_rationale: The permanent nature of these incentives represents a significant commitment to enhancing human capital in Italy.
reforms.source_quote: Tax incentives on the repatriation of researchers have been made permanent.

reforms.description: The Italian government approved a comprehensive education reform called 'La buona scuola' in July 2015, which aims to enhance school autonomy, improve teacher recruitment, and introduce performance-based salary components for teachers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the quality of education and aligning skills with labor market needs, the reform is expected to enhance employability and productivity in the long term.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was passed and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the education system, aiming to significantly improve educational outcomes and align them with labor market demands, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Italian government approved a comprehensive education reform – 'La buona scuola' – on July 2015.

reforms.description: The Italian government has introduced a National Industry 4.0 Plan, providing approximately EUR 13 billion in incentives to enhance innovation and skills in new technologies from 2017 to 2020. This plan focuses on developing specialist digital skills through university courses and higher technical institutes, as well as funding research and creating digital innovation hubs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan aims to modernize the economy by boosting innovation and enhancing the skills of the workforce, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been launched with allocated funding for innovation and skills development.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan represents a significant structural change aimed at modernizing Italy's economy and addressing skill shortages in critical areas, making it a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: In 2016, the government launched the National Industry 4.0 Plan, which provides a range of incentives (for about EUR 13 billion) to boost innovation and skills in new technologies over 2017-20.

reforms.description: Italy has introduced a wider range of incentives to boost innovation under the Industry 4.0 Plan for 2017-20, including reinforced R&D tax credits and a hyper- and super-depreciation scheme to promote digitalisation.
reforms.component_name: R&D Tax Credits and Depreciation Schemes
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are designed to enhance corporate investment in R&D and digitalisation, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax credits were reinforced in the 2017 budget law.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in Italy's approach to innovation policy, aligning it more closely with other OECD countries and aiming to stimulate substantial increases in R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: Italy has recently introduced a wider range of incentives to boost innovation under the Industry 4.0 Plan over 2017-20.

reforms.description: The Good School reform aims to enhance Italy's education system by granting more autonomy to schools, introducing merit-based bonuses for teachers, and mandating school-to-work experiences for students. This initiative is expected to improve educational outcomes and strengthen links between education and the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving education quality and aligning it with labor market needs, the reform is expected to enhance the skills of the future workforce, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was passed and is currently being implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant overhaul of the education system, aiming to improve educational outcomes and better prepare students for the labor market, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Good School reform can drastically improve Italy’s education system.

reforms.description: Italy's research and innovation policy has been hindered by excessive fragmentation, with multiple national research funds and unclear changes in legislation affecting program coherence and alignment with national priorities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A coherent and well-funded research and innovation policy can enhance productivity and innovation capacity, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is aware of the fragmentation and has proposed a national strategy to address it.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to unify and streamline Italy's research funding landscape, which is critical for enhancing the overall effectiveness of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: Italy’s research and innovation policy has for a long time suffered from excessive fragmentation.

reforms.description: The government has proposed a balance between R&D tax credits and direct support measures to enhance R&D expenditure, particularly for young firms lacking necessary funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing targeted support to young firms, the reform aims to stimulate innovation and increase overall R&D investment, which can drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal includes a serious monitoring and evaluation system to ensure effective implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how Italy supports R&D, aiming to create a more conducive environment for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The right balance between R&D tax credits and direct support measures is crucial for enhancing R&D expenditure.

reforms.description: The National Research Programme (PNR) was introduced to outline Italy's national research strategy for 2015-2020, focusing on improving governance, internationalisation, and public-private collaboration in research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The PNR aims to enhance research quality and efficiency, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The PNR has been established and is being executed as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: The PNR represents a significant overhaul of Italy's research strategy, aiming for long-term improvements in governance and collaboration, which are crucial for enhancing the country's innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: In 2015, the Ministry of Education, University and Research published the National Research Programme outlining the national research strategy over the next five years.

reforms.description: Italy introduced an R&D tax credit system to incentivize collaboration between businesses and universities or research institutes, aiming to enhance R&D investment and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit is designed to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax credit system was introduced and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the R&D tax credit is a significant step towards enhancing private sector R&D investment, although it is part of a broader set of reforms.
reforms.source_quote: In 2015, the government introduced a tax credit for R&D collaboration between businesses and universities or research institutes.

reforms.description: The introduction of a patent box regime provides a lower tax rate for income generated by intellectual property rights, aiming to incentivize innovation and attract investment in R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While the patent box aims to encourage innovation, its effectiveness is debated, particularly regarding its benefits for start-ups and SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The patent box regime was established and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The patent box regime represents a significant policy tool for incentivizing innovation, although its actual impact on start-ups and SMEs may be limited.
reforms.source_quote: The 2015 Stability Law also introduced a patent box, which is a lower tax regime applying to income generated by intellectual property rights.

reforms.description: The Italian government has implemented measures to streamline and accelerate insolvency and foreclosure procedures, including the 'Marcian Pact' which simplifies the transfer of real estate collateral in case of missed repayments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the efficiency of insolvency procedures, the reform aims to enhance credit supply to startups and SMEs, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken important steps in these directions by streamlining and accelerating insolvency and foreclosure procedures.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at improving the financial ecosystem for startups and SMEs, which are crucial for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Major initiatives include: shortening the period for tax deductibility of loan losses from 5 years to 1 year, in line with other EU countries; establishing a government guarantee scheme on senior tranches of securitised non-performing loans...

reforms.description: The Italian government has established a fund called InvitaliaVenture to co-invest with national and international private investors in highly innovative start-ups and SMEs, aiming to develop the venture capital industry in Italy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating private investment in innovative start-ups, the fund aims to enhance the overall venture capital landscape, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a co-investment fund represents a significant structural change in the venture capital landscape, aiming to stimulate innovation and investment in high-growth sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The government has recently established a fund (InvitaliaVenture) to co-invest with national and international private investors in highly innovative start-ups and SMEs.

reforms.description: The Italian government has introduced tax advantages and streamlined procedures for unlisted SMEs to issue minibonds, aiming to improve access to capital markets for small and medium enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to capital for SMEs, the minibond initiative is expected to enhance their financial stability and capacity for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Tax advantages and streamlined procedures have been introduced for minibonds.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards improving SME access to finance, which is crucial for fostering innovation and growth.
reforms.source_quote: Tax advantages and streamlined procedures to issue bonds by unlisted SMEs (minibonds) have been introduced.
